comment_text.ud6.7:4.4: For “they never return” (na jātu’m’eti) there is a variant reading “go to rebirth” (jātim eti) in Śarīrārthagāthā 32, which here is very close to the Pali. The commentary also acknowledges this variant: ‘Na jāti metī’tipi paṭhanti, so evattho.

root_text.ud6.7:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud6.7:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā subhūti bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya avitakkaṁ samādhiṁ samāpajjitvā.
root_text.ud6.7:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ subhūtiṁ avidūre nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya avitakkaṁ samādhiṁ samāpannaṁ.
root_text.ud6.7:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud6.7:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud6.7:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Subhūti was sitting not far from the Buddha, cross-legged, with his body straight, having attained the immersion free of placing the mind.
translation_text.ud6.7:2.1: The Buddha saw him meditating there.
translation_text.ud6.7:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud6.7:4.1: “In whom mental vibrations are cleared away,
translation_text.ud6.7:4.4: having overcome the four yokes, <j>they never return.”

variant_text.ud6.7:4.4: Catuyogātigato na jātu metī”ti → na jātiṁ etīti (sya-all); na jātimetīti (aṭṭhakathāyaṁ. pāṭhantaraṁ)
